questions.question: Which of the following functions has an inverse?
Find the inverse of $f(x) = 3x - 2$. Let $f^{-1}(x)$ be the inverse function.
If $f(x) = 2x$ and $g(x) = \frac{x}{2}$, which statement is true?

Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has $120 in his savings account. He wants to buy a bike that costs $180. If Tom's savings account earns interest at a rate of $f(x) = 0.05x$, how much will he have after 1 year?
Which of the following statements about inverse functions is true?

options.A: The domain of the inverse function is the same as the range of the original function.
options.B: The range of the inverse function is the same as the domain of the original function.
options.C: The domain and range of the inverse function are the same as the domain and range of the original function.
options.D: The inverse function is always a linear function.
options.E: The inverse function is never a linear function.

questions.question: A water tank can be filled at a rate of $f(x) = 2x$ gallons per minute. If $g(x)$ is the inverse function of $f(x)$, what does $g(x)$ represent?

options.A: The amount of time it takes to fill the tank with x gallons of water.
options.B: The amount of water in the tank after x minutes.
options.C: The rate at which the tank is filled.
options.E: The amount of water that can be filled in x minutes.

questions.question: A company has a function $f(x)$ that represents the cost of producing x units of a product. If the inverse function is $f^{-1}(x)$, what does $f^{-1}(x)$ represent?

options.A: The number of units produced at a cost of x dollars.
options.B: The cost of producing x units.
options.C: The profit made from selling x units.
options.D: The number of units sold at a price of x dollars.
options.E: The revenue generated from selling x units.

questions.question: Which of the following is a real-world application of inverse functions?

options.A: Calculating the area of a circle.
options.B: Finding the inverse of a linear function.
options.C: Determining the cost of producing x units of a product.

questions.question: A person is planning a road trip from City A to City B. The distance between the two cities is 240 miles, and the person wants to drive at an average speed of 40 miles per hour. If $f(x)$ represents the time it takes to drive x miles, find the inverse function $f^{-1}(x)$ and interpret its meaning in the context of the problem. Show all work and explain your answer.

A company has a function $f(x) = 2x + 1$ that represents the amount of money (in dollars) a person spends on groceries per week. Find the inverse function $f^{-1}(x)$ and use it to determine how much a person spends if they buy $12 worth of groceries. Show all work and explain your answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to check their work by plugging their answers back into the original equation.
Tip 2: Remind students that the inverse of a function is not always a function itself.
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of understanding the context and interpreting the results in real-world applications.
